data description: Garmin GPS waypoint number associated with the individual observation.
Concatenated or compact site/subsite label for the observation location.
Individual opihi identifier within the morphology observation table.
Field classification of whether the individual was in refuge, exposed, or intermediate habitat.
Whether the individual was observed moving; values are y/n.
Raw shell length measurement; analysis script converts this from inches to centimeters.
Raw shell width measurement; analysis script converts this from inches to centimeters.
Raw shell height measurement; analysis script converts this from inches to centimeters.
Whether there was an apparent path to open ocean water; values are y/n.
Surface angle at the observation point, probably in degrees.
Signed distance to open water; units appear to be feet based on notes.
Signed distance to nearest shelter feature; units appear to be feet based on notes.
Signed distance to under-rock shelter; may include nonnumeric codes such as toofar.
Type of shelter feature recorded when shelter was present.
Distance to the feature or taxon recorded as Littpint; exact meaning is not documented.
Signed distance to crustose substrate or algae; units appear to be feet based on notes.
Compass bearing related to surface orientation, in degrees.
Compass bearing toward ocean direction, in degrees.
Photo reference or image sequence identifier; some values may have spreadsheet date autoformatting.
Color code or description for shell ribs.
Shell erosion score or categorical code.
Color code or description for shell inter-rib areas.
Whether the rock/substrate could be moved; values are y/n.
Substrate material or substrate class at the observation point.
Free-text field notes and corrections.
Whether limu/algae was present on the shell; values are y/n.
Field position or microhabitat descriptor on the transect.
Hypotenuse measurement used for field geometry or slope calculations; exact method not documented.
Altitude or vertical leg measurement used with Hypot/Angle; exact method not documented.
Angle measurement associated with field geometry or slope.
Coded shore type classification.
